title: Dissociation of Jejunum cells for clumps sorting
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9nkk0","text":"Single-cell RNA sequencing combined with spatial information on landmark genes enables the reconstruction of spatially-resolved tissue cell atlases. However, such approaches are challenging for rare cell types since their mRNA contents are diluted in the spatial transcriptomics bulk measurements used for landmark gene detection. To overcome the limitations in reconstructing spatial expression profiles of rare cells, we present ClumpSeq, an approach for sequencing small clumps of attached tissue cells. Sequencing clumps increase the capture rate of rare cell types without the need for antibody enrichment and utilizes the spatial information of the major tissue cell type. We use this approach to reconstruct spatial maps of all intestinal secretory epithelial cell types along the crypt-villus axis, revealing zonated immune-modulatory programs and heterogeneous migration patterns. ClumpSeq can be applied for reconstructing spatial atlases of rare cell types in other tissues and tumors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare buffers: Buffer1 (25ml): %s %s DPBS (-/-) 24.5 mlEDTA 0.5M (10mM) 500 µl Buffer2 (50ml): For Hoechst %s %s DMEM high glucose 44,5 ml10% FBS 5 mlHepes 1M (10mM) 500 µl
Set a centrifuge to 4 °C .
Warm at 37 °C 15 mL of buffer1 and 5 mL of buffer2.
Prepare a tube with cold DPBS and put it On ice .
Thaw the FBS. IMPORTANT: The whole procedure needs to be done on ice!!!.
Collect the tissue: proximal Jejunum - take 8 cm of tissue, starting 8 cm distal of the stomach.
Wash the tissue: use a syringe filled with DPBS to flush the inside.
Lateralize the tissue to flat it.
Remove adjacent fat.
Incubate the tissue for 00:20:00 in buffer1 On ice .
Move the tissue in the pre-warmed buffer1 and cut it into small pieces (~ 1 cm ).
Add 1 U of DNase I in the tube.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at 37 °C - shake every minute.
Filter the solution through a 100 μm mesh.
Spin down in the cold centrifuge ( 4 °C ) for 00:05:00 at 300 g .
Discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in 8 mL cold DPBS + 2 mL FBS and use the pipette to break the pellet gently.
Filter the solution through 100 μm mesh.
Spin down in the cold centrifuge ( 4 °C ) for 00:05:00 at 300 g .
Discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in 5 mL of pre-warmed buffer2 with Hoechst (1:500) + Reserpine (1:4000) for 00:05:00 a Room temperature (keep it in the dark).
Spin down in a cold centrifuge ( 4 °C ) for 00:05:00 at 300 g .
Discard supernatant.
Resuspend in DPBS and add 1:500 Zombie for 00:15:00 Room temperature (Zombie works only in DPBS!!!).
Discard the supernatant.
Resuspend the pellet in FACS buffer (accordingly to the pellet). The sample is ready for the FACS sorting!!!
creator: Rita Manco
